linting.notes: Ruff is configured via tox.ini. The lint command runs both 'ruff check' and 'ruff format'. CI enforces that linting produces no changes (git diff must be clean after lint).

testing.mock_patterns: Uses moto library to mock AWS S3 services (@mock_aws decorator)
testing.notes: Only unit tests exist (tests/unit/test_s3.py with 3 tests). Tests use moto to mock AWS services, so no external infrastructure is required. Pytest config is in tox.ini with 'addopts = -ra' and 'testpaths = tests'.

gating_checks.name: Lint
gating_checks.notes: Runs ruff check and format. Fails if git state is dirty after linting.

gating_checks.name: Mypy
gating_checks.notes: Type checking with mypy on src/ directory.

advisory_checks.name: End-to-End Server Startup
advisory_checks.notes: Matrix build across ubuntu-latest/macos-latest and local/remote modes. Has 'continue-on-error: true', so not a gating check. Tests server startup and health endpoint.

ci.notes: All gating checks trigger on push to main and pull_request to main. Python 3.11 is used consistently across all workflows.

build.notes: Project uses setuptools with pyproject.toml. Source code is in src/ directory. No explicit build step needed - just install with pip. Containerfile uses python:3.11-slim base and requires git system dependency.

conventions.test_naming: Functions starting with 'test_' inside files named 'test_*.py'
conventions.notes: Project follows standard Python testing conventions. Tests are organized under tests/unit/. Uses type hints with mypy validation.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

lint_commands.notes: Exit code 0. All checks passed, 10 files left unchanged.

test_commands.notes: Exit code 0. 3 passed, 3 warnings in 4.50s. Warnings are from pydantic and deprecated imports, not test failures.
Exit code 0. Success: no issues found in 7 source files.

container_recipe.notes: All tox environments create isolated virtualenvs under .tox/. The test environment must be set up first (via 'tox -e test') before using pytest directly. No special environment variables required for unit tests.

validation.summary: All validation steps passed. Install OK, lint OK (no issues), mypy OK (no issues), tests OK (3/3 passed). Container recipe is fully functional.

gaps: No integration tests or end-to-end tests that run in standard CI (e2e workflow has continue-on-error)
No coverage measurement configured (no coverage threshold or reporting)
Only 3 unit tests total - test coverage appears minimal
E2E tests require Llama Stack server setup and may need external resources
readiness_justification: All lint and test commands validated successfully in a clean container. Dependencies install without issues. Tests pass reliably with clear exit codes. No external infrastructure required for unit tests (moto mocks AWS). An agent can clone, patch, lint, test, and get unambiguous pass/fail signals.
